what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment of type images in the design-assets channel, pointing to an image asset at image://design-assets/icons/User_Portal.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target uses an image:// URI scheme pointing to design-assets/icons/User_Portal
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset +00:00
No body content beyond the frontmatter
standouts: Uses a custom image:// URI to locate the asset
Attachment id att-ac9574a8 follows a channel-attachments naming convention
concerns: Target URI is not resolvable from the file alone; no size, format, or checksum metadata
notes: A lightweight metadata stub linking a design-assets channel attachment to a User Portal icon image.
questions: What does image:// resolve to in storage?
Is the target path stable or versioned?
Are there other attachment types with the same schema?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an attachment ledger entry; its content matches that purpose, though it lacks asset-level details.
intention_signals: It is trying to register an image asset in a channel so that channel memory can recall and reference it by a stable id and URI.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme for asset location, which is not further defined in the file.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped attachment record that maps a channel-scoped id to an image asset URI, enabling retrieval and referencing of design assets by identity rather than path.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file for the design-assets channel that registers an images attachment pointing to image://design-assets/icons/User_Portal with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog and reference a User Portal icon image asset within the design-assets channel memory.
